payload.title: [REF-001] Decompose task-engine-internal megamodule
technicalScope: Split src/modules/task-engine/task-engine-internal.ts into task-engine/commands/* (or agreed) handler modules.
Keep taskEngineModule export and workspace-kit run JSON contracts behavior-identical.
Run full pnpm run test and spot-check representative wk run commands (schema-only where appropriate).
acceptanceCriteria: Dispatch file is intentionally thin (registration + routing only); clustered logic lives outside it.
pnpm run build and pnpm run test pass; no intentional command JSON regressions.

metadata.issue: task-engine-internal.ts aggregates the entire task-engine onCommand surface in one megamodule, raising defect and merge-risk.
metadata.supportingReasoning: Line-count and coupling review identified a single dominant file (~2.7k+ lines) as primary maintainability bottleneck; aligns with src/README layering.
proposedSolutions: Per-command-family handlers imported by slim task-engine-internal.ts dispatch

payload.title: [REF-002] Decompose context-activation index megamodule
technicalScope: Split modules/context-activation/onCommand surface into handlers/* with thin index.ts registration.
Extract shared pagination helpers to a dedicated file where duplicated.
Preserve CAE CLI JSON outputs and imports per module boundary rules.
acceptanceCriteria: context-activation/index.ts is primarily composition and routing.
Build and tests green; schema-only smoke for representative cae-* commands.

metadata.issue: context-activation/index.ts mixes CAE evaluation, SQLite, guidance, and pagination in one very large module entry.
metadata.supportingReasoning: ~1.5k line single-file entry complicates isolated changes and parallel maintenance; refactor-proposal pack documents extraction plan.

payload.title: [REF-003] Unify kit SQLite planning persistence kernel
technicalScope: Introduce shared core/state kit-sqlite open+migrate primitives incrementally.
Converge cae-kit-sqlite and sqlite-dual-planning callers without changing on-disk paths by default.
Maintain compatibility re-exports; extend migration/fixture coverage per RELEASING gates.
acceptanceCriteria: parity/check-compatibility and tests pass as required for persistence-touching work;
Single documented user_version bump story; doctor/readiness parity spot-check.

metadata.issue: Planning-related SQLite DDL and open paths are fragmented across workspace-kit-sqlite, cae-kit-sqlite, and task-engine persistence, risking drift.
metadata.supportingReasoning: Architectural review identified overlapping migrations and callers; phased kernel reduces duplicate PRAGMA/migration omissions.

payload.title: [REF-004] Enforce module boundaries and shared validation ports
technicalScope: Inventory sibling-module imports under modules/* (e.g. task-engine vs skills validation).
Relocate pure shared validation to core or contracts; optionally add CI script allowlist gate.
Update src/README.md documented exceptions.
acceptanceCriteria: No unauthorized sibling imports or explicit allowlisted exceptions with rationale;

metadata.issue: Stated module layering (modules depend on core/contracts only) is violated by concrete imports such as task-engine to skills validation.
metadata.supportingReasoning: Preserves architectural predictability during megamodule refactors; proposal links core/planning facade pattern.

payload.title: [REF-005] Split planning module index megamodule
technicalScope: Extract helpers from modules/planning/index.ts into cohesive files (plan-output, execution-drafts, id utils).
Keep WorkflowModule wiring in index.ts;
Confirm build-plan semantics and strict planning policies unchanged.

metadata.issue: modules/planning/index.ts combines interview logic, drafts, outputs, and task-engine coupling in one large surface.
metadata.supportingReasoning: ~900 LOC single file complicates phased build-plan edits; refactor-proposal proposes extraction boundaries.

payload.title: [REF-006] Restructure core/cae into domain subfolders
technicalScope: Create core/cae subfolders (registry, persistence, evaluation, guidance) with incremental moves;
Use re-export shims as needed;
Update imports in CLI and context-activation.
acceptanceCriteria: Build and tests pass; mechanical move-only;

metadata.issue: core/cae is a flat swarm of peers; navigability and ownership unclear.
metadata.supportingReasoning: Structural grouping reduces grep-only discovery burden; aligns with REF-002 handler split.

payload.title: [REF-007] Layer workspace-kit CLI (cli run doctor policy helpers)
technicalScope: Extract pure helpers from cli.ts and cli/run-command.ts;
Aggregate doctor collation under cli/doctor where applicable;
Preserve WorkspaceKitCliOptions and exit semantics.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests plus smoke checklist in PR;

metadata.issue: cli.ts and run-command.ts mix bootstrap doctor run pipeline policy checkpoints.
metadata.supportingReasoning: Separation lowers regression blast radius when changing wk run only.

payload.title: [REF-008] Split config stack (config-cli metadata resolution)
technicalScope: Partition config-metadata.ts by facet merges;
Keep config-cli as orchestration only;
Avoid silent JSON explanation shape breaks per semver norms.

metadata.issue: config-cli workspace-kit-config config-metadata overlap inflates deltas for single knobs.
metadata.supportingReasoning: Facet-oriented split matches registry model and refactor-proposal structure.

payload.title: [REF-009] Align adapters stub with SQLite integration reality
technicalScope: Choose Direction A adapters B retire C shim per refactor proposal;
Coordinate optional REF-003 seams;
Update src/README.md layering honesty.

metadata.issue: src/adapters is nearly empty whereas persistence lives under core/state and task-engine.
metadata.supportingReasoning: False architectural promise wastes onboarding time;

payload.title: [REF-010] Narrow package exports vs modules barrel re-exports
technicalScope: Audit programmatic consumers of npm package;
Additive package exports subpaths deprecation before removal;
Document semver stable API stance.

metadata.issue: Wide modules barrel re-exports ambiguous semver-bound public API for library consumers.
metadata.supportingReasoning: package.json partial exports precedent should extend intentionally;
